pr.body:
## Summary

Fixes #1387

When Claude authentication has expired, the `/limits` command previously displayed empty subsection headers below the error message:

**Before:**
```
Claude limits
Claude authentication expired. Please use `/solve` or `/hive` commands to trigger re-authentication of Claude.

Claude 5 hour session

Current week (all models)

Current week (Sonnet only)
```

**After:**
```
Claude limits
Claude authentication expired. Please use `/solve` or `/hive` commands to trigger re-authentication of Claude.
```

## Root Cause

In `formatUsageMessage()` in `src/limits.lib.mjs`, when `claudeError` is set, the code rendered three empty subsection headers because each content branch checked `if (claudeError)` and skipped actual data — but the headers were still emitted before those branches.

Additionally, the `/limits` handler in `telegram-bot.mjs` used `message.lastIndexOf('```')` to inject the queue status string into the already-assembled message — fragile string surgery that could easily break if the format changed.

## Fix

**`src/limits.lib.mjs`** — `formatUsageMessage` now builds an array of section strings and joins them all into a single code block at the very end:
- Each section (CPU, RAM, Disk, GitHub, Claude) is an independent string pushed into a `sections` array.
- When `claudeError` is set, only the single Claude-error section is pushed (no subsection headers).
- A new optional `extraSections` parameter lets callers inject additional sections (e.g. queue status) before the closing code block is formed — no string searching required.

**`src/telegram-bot.mjs`** — The `/limits` handler now:
1. Awaits `solveQueue.formatStatus()` before calling `formatUsageMessage`.
2. Passes the queue status as `extraSections: [queueStatus]`.
3. The `lastIndexOf` / `slice` string-surgery block is removed entirely.

## Tests

Updated and expanded `tests/limits-display.test.mjs` (38 tests, all passing):
- Asserts empty subsection headers are NOT included when `claudeError` is present (original fix).
- Asserts `formatUsageMessage` output is wrapped in **exactly one** code block.
- Asserts `extraSections` content appears inside the code block (before the closing fence) in the normal path.
- Asserts `extraSections` content appears inside the code block when `claudeError` is set.
- Asserts empty `extraSections` produces identical output to the default (no regressions).
- Asserts sections are separated by blank lines.

All 299 tests across all test files pass.

---
*This PR was created automatically by the AI issue solver*

Will it not break queue display that goes after claude limits section?

🤖 **AI Work Session Started**

Starting automated work session at 2026-03-04T09:51:48.408Z

The PR has been converted to draft mode while work is in progress.

_This comment marks the beginning of an AI work session. Please wait for the session to finish, and provide your feedback._

Good question! The queue display is **not** broken.

In `telegram-bot.mjs` (line 794-797), the queue status is injected by finding the **last** ``` in the message:

```javascript
const codeBlockEnd = message.lastIndexOf('```');
if (codeBlockEnd !== -1) {
  const queueStatus = await solveQueue.formatStatus();
  message = message.slice(0, codeBlockEnd) + `\n${queueStatus}` + message.slice(codeBlockEnd);
}
```

Both the normal path and the error path (early return) end with a closing ```, so `lastIndexOf('```')` correctly finds it in both cases.

I verified this with a quick test:

**With `claudeError` + queue injection:**
```
```
Current time: Mar 4, 9:53am UTC

Claude limits
Claude authentication expired. Please use /solve or /hive commands.

Queue: 0 pending, 1 processing```
```

The queue status is inserted before the closing backticks, as expected. All 41 tests pass.

---
*Now working session is ended, feel free to review and add any feedback on the solution draft.*

Make sure we have all necessary automated tests to ensure everything is working. Also instead of finding three backticks to insert next section, make sure we form an array of sections, and once we have all of them we finally put them all into code block, so there will be no confusing logic in the future.

🤖 **AI Work Session Started**

Starting automated work session at 2026-03-04T10:04:23.344Z

The PR has been converted to draft mode while work is in progress.

_This comment marks the beginning of an AI work session. Please wait for the session to finish, and provide your feedback._

Addressed the feedback from the previous comment:

**1. Sections-array approach** (`src/limits.lib.mjs`): `formatUsageMessage` now builds an array of section strings and joins them all into a single code block at the end. No more string accumulation with early-returns that left the code block half-open, and no more fragile `lastIndexOf('```')` surgery.

**2. Queue status injection** (`src/telegram-bot.mjs`): Queue status is now fetched before `formatUsageMessage` is called and passed via the new `extraSections` parameter. The `lastIndexOf` / `slice` block is gone entirely.

**3. Tests** (`tests/limits-display.test.mjs`): Added 5 new tests (38 total, all passing):
- Single code block (exactly 2 fences)
- `extraSections` content inside code block — normal path
- `extraSections` content inside code block — `claudeError` path  
- Empty `extraSections` parity (no regression)
- Blank-line separation between sections

All 299 tests across all test files pass locally.

commits.messageBody:
Adding CLAUDE.md with task information for AI processing.
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1387

…s output

When Claude authentication has expired, the /limits command previously
rendered empty subsection headers (Claude 5 hour session, Current week
all models, Current week Sonnet only) below the error message. These
sections contained no data so they were visually noisy.

Now, when a claudeError is present, the message closes after showing
"Claude limits\n<error message>" without rendering the empty subsections.

Fixes #1387

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.messageBody:
…ndexOf queue injection

- formatUsageMessage now builds content as an array of section strings and wraps
  them all in a single code block at the end, instead of accumulating a string
  with an early-return code-block and post-hoc string surgery
- Added extraSections parameter to formatUsageMessage so callers can inject
  additional sections (e.g. queue status) before the closing code block
- /limits handler in telegram-bot.mjs now passes queue status as an extraSection
  instead of using lastIndexOf('```') to splice content into the message string
- Added 5 new tests covering: single code block, extraSections in normal path,
  extraSections in claudeError path, empty extraSections parity, blank-line separation

Fixes: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1387

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
